feedback.text: Please extend this initiative to video cameras as well. For years, boycott publishers voluntarily use this industry to ensure that no one owns more the one they buy. For example, games that, even if purchased in physical form, without an online check (which can be removed by the publisher) cannot function, or even games that leave incomplete and to function, must be downloaded from the operator’s servers that have a limited duration. Everything to make sure that the consumer plans the consumer is accustomed to paying permanently for the use of software and moving away from the practice of one-off purchase. Videogames today cost much more than in the past, but despite this they only want to sell a licence to us at their own discretion, which can last 10 years as 3 months, if the game does not prove to be profitable, makes it inaccessible and even reimburses consumers, unless they give us a lawyer. The situation is serious, we are still in time to ensure that it does not end up as in the PC software market, where almost every program is now sold only at odd prices.
